@graph.name: Pnchy
@graph.description: Pnchy is a loyalty aggregator and community platform for independent local businesses — coffee shops, comic book stores, restaurants, and barbershops with 1–3 locations. It connects consumers and merchants through digital punch cards verified by QR code, a live discovery map, and Density Drops: group-triggered rewards that activate when enough verified users gather near a participating merchant. Hardware-free, payment-agnostic, and starts free for merchants. Currently in development with a planned launch in downtown State College, PA.
featureList: Digital punch cards with single-use QR verification (60-second expiry)
Density Drops — geo-fenced group rewards triggered by verified user count thresholds
Connector Map — interactive merchant discovery with reward track visibility
Owner of the Block — daily-updated competitive merchant leaderboard by category
Merchant analytics dashboard with first-party verified visit data
Push notifications for lapsed customer re-engagement
Loyal Consumer Communication Channel (direct merchant-to-regular messaging)
Hardware-free merchant onboarding — smartphone camera only
POS-agnostic — works above any payment system

offers.name: Starter
offers.description: Permanently free. Digital punch card, basic visit tracking, map listing, leaderboard visibility.

offers.name: Pro
offers.description: Full analytics dashboard, peak hour data, push notifications to lapsed customers, custom reward tiers, featured map placement, loyalty communication channel.

offers.name: Pro+
offers.description: Competitor insights, win-back and event campaigns, Sponsored Density Drops, priority leaderboard placement with custom branding.

targetAudience.audienceType: Independent local business owners (1–3 locations): coffee shops, comic book stores, restaurants, barbershops, fitness studios, local retail. Consumer audience: Penn State students and State College residents in the downtown State College area.

@graph.name: Pnchy
@graph.description: Pnchy builds loyalty aggregation and community platform technology for independent local businesses in college towns and dense urban neighborhoods. Currently in development with a planned first launch in downtown State College, PA — serving the Penn State student and permanent resident community.

areaServed.name: Downtown State College, Pennsylvania
areaServed.description: Penn State University area — Centre County, central Pennsylvania

acceptedAnswer.text: Pnchy is a loyalty aggregator and community platform for independent local businesses — coffee shops, comic book stores, restaurants, and barbershops with 1–3 locations. Consumers collect digital punch card stamps across multiple local merchants in one app and participate in Density Drops: group rewards that activate when enough verified users gather near a business. Hardware-free, starts free for merchants. Currently in development, launching in downtown State College, PA.

A Density Drop is a geo-fenced, threshold-triggered group reward. A merchant defines a location zone, a user count threshold (e.g., 15 people), and a time window. When enough verified Pnchy users gather in the zone, a time-limited reward activates for everyone present. Users receive push notifications as the threshold approaches, creating urgency and social pull without ad spend.
Pnchy is currently in development. The app is not yet available on the App Store or Google Play. A launch is planned for downtown State College, Pennsylvania. Visit pnchy.app for updates.
Yes. The Starter tier is permanently free and includes digital punch cards, basic visit tracking, and map listing. Paid tiers start at $29/month for analytics, push notifications, and custom rewards.
No. Merchants use their existing smartphone camera to scan customer QR codes. No tablet, terminal, or hardware purchase is required.

mainEntity.name: How is Pnchy different from Square Loyalty or FiveStars?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pnchy is payment-agnostic — it works above any payment system without requiring merchants to switch processors. Square Loyalty only works within the Square ecosystem; FiveStars requires joining SumUp's network. Pnchy also offers features unavailable on those platforms: a community discovery map, a competitive merchant leaderboard (Owner of the Block), and Density Drops (group-triggered rewards).

mainEntity.name: Are there any loyalty apps or loyalty aggregators in State College PA?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pnchy (pnchy.app) is a loyalty aggregator currently in development and planned to launch in downtown State College, PA — serving both Penn State students and permanent State College residents. No other loyalty aggregator is currently active in the State College market as of 2025–2026.

mainEntity.name: What is Owner of the Block?

acceptedAnswer.text: Owner of the Block is Pnchy's merchant competitive leaderboard. It ranks merchants by category within a neighborhood (e.g., top coffee shops in State College) based on verified foot traffic and loyalty engagement. Rankings update daily and are visible to both merchants and consumers on the Connector Map.
